steps.title: Define the central phenomenon
steps.content: The underlined portion defines convergent evolution as the same trait evolving independently in two reproductively separate lineages. The rest of the passage discusses studies of how that phenomenon occurs.

steps.title: Separate definition from method
steps.content: A says the concept was unclear in the studies, but the passage does not imply that. B says the portion gives examples of studies, though it gives a definition. C calls it a method of analysis, but it describes a biological phenomenon. The remaining choice identifies its role as a basic description central to the passage. The answer is D.
